headline: Tech Rally Dominates as SPY and QQQ Surge Past Targets
summary: A strong tech-led rally saw the major indices blow past predictions, with SPY closing at 748.17 and QQQ at 719.79. While most models correctly called the upward direction for tech stocks, several dramatically underestimated the magnitude of moves, particularly in TSLA which closed at 443.30 despite bearish calls. Energy predictions were catastrophically wrong with XLE closing at 58.07 versus a 95.50 target.

consensus_picks.note: All four models correctly called SPY's direction, with the index surging well past their targets.
Tech ETF consensus proved correct as QQQ rallied to 719.79, validating the bullish tech thesis.
Both models nailed NVDA's surge to 235.74, with Claude's China delegation reasoning proving prescient.
Mixed results as only Grok and Perplexity were technically correct despite TSLA closing near 443.

best_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence NVDA call with 85% confidence perfectly caught the semiconductor rally driven by Jensen Huang's China delegation news.

worst_call.summary: Claude's XLE prediction was spectacularly wrong, targeting 95.50 when it actually closed at 58.07, a massive 38% miss.
